evals.name: youtube-manual-sub-subtitle-first
evals.prompt: I want to read this YouTube talk: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ . The video has human-authored English subtitles. Get me the transcript as plain text. What's the fastest, most accurate way and what exact command should I run?
evals.expected_output: Recommends the subtitle-first approach: probe with yt-dlp --list-subs for a MANUAL subtitle, and since one exists, extract it with scripts/yt_subs_to_txt.py (near-instant, near-zero error) rather than downloading audio and running an ASR pipeline. Should NOT recommend transcribing the audio when a manual subtitle is available, and should distinguish manual subtitles from auto-captions.

assertions.description: Does not jump straight to an audio ASR pipeline

evals.name: mandarin-local-qwen3-opencc
evals.prompt: I have a local Mandarin Chinese lecture recording at ~/audio/lecture01.m4a. It's literary, with idioms and several proper nouns (names of people and organizations), and occasional English words mixed in. I want an accurate Traditional Chinese transcript. Which engine and exact flags should I use?
evals.expected_output: Recommends mlx-qwen3-asr with the Qwen3-ASR-1.7B model (NOT 0.6B, which mangles idioms), --language Chinese to force language ID across code-switching, --context with a glossary of the proper nouns as the highest-leverage flag, and a post-process with opencc -c s2twp.json for Simplified->Traditional. Must explicitly warn AGAINST enabling speculative --draft-model decoding.

assertions.description: The recommended command does not enable --draft-model
assertions.description_long: Any command block recommended for running should not contain an enabled --draft-model flag.

evals.name: english-local-apple-silicon-parakeet-v2-trap
evals.prompt: I'm on an Apple Silicon MacBook and have a local English lecture recording at ~/audio/talk.mp3. I want the most accurate English transcript. Which engine should I use and what's the exact command? Are there any gotchas?
evals.expected_output: Recommends parakeet-mlx on Apple Silicon, and CRITICALLY flags that the CLI default model is v3 (multilingual) which is worse on English than v2, so the command must pass --model mlx-community/parakeet-tdt-0.6b-v2 explicitly. May offer mlx-whisper large-v3-turbo as the proper-noun-friendly alternative, with a warning that a long initial_prompt can cause hallucination loops.

assertions.description: Mentions mlx-whisper turbo alternative or initial_prompt caveat

evals.name: which-engine-for-my-machine-routing
evals.prompt: I have a folder of audio files to transcribe locally and I'm not sure which whisper engine is right for my hardware. How do I figure out the best engine for my machine, and how do I run a resumable batch that won't redo work if it gets interrupted?
evals.expected_output: Directs the user to run scripts/detect_backend.py to detect OS/arch/GPU and get the recommended engine (mlx-whisper on Apple Silicon, faster-whisper cuda on NVIDIA, faster-whisper cpu/int8 otherwise), and to use scripts/transcribe_batch.py with --engine auto for a resumable batch that skips already-transcribed files and writes a ledger. Should reference running via uv.
